title: Fix config tests using full day names instead of abbreviations
description: config_test.go lines 298 and 344 use 'monday', 'tuesday' etc instead of 'mon', 'tue'. These tests pass because they only check config loading, not schedule activation. Fix to use correct abbreviations.
close_reason: Fixed in f79cca1: tests now use abbreviated day names (mon, tue, etc)

title: BUG: No validation of schedule time format - panic at runtime
description: parseTime() in schedule.go panics on malformed HH:MM strings. No validation exists in config loading. User writing start: 'nine' or start: '' gets a runtime panic. Fix: validate start/end match HH:MM in validateSchedules().
close_reason: Fixed in f79cca1: time format validated with HH:MM regex during config load

title: BUG: Schedule with empty appSet silently accepted
description: validateSchedules skips appSet validation when empty. A schedule with no appSet passes load, then fails at runtime when the window activates. Fix: reject empty appSet during validation.
close_reason: Fixed in f79cca1: empty appSet now returns error during config load

title: BUG: Day names not validated - 'monday' silently never matches
description: schedule.go uses 3-letter abbreviations (mon, tue, etc) but config loading never validates day names. A user writing 'monday' gets a config that loads fine but never activates. The config test at config_test.go:298 even uses full day names, masking this bug. Fix: validate day names in validateSchedules(), fix test YAML.
close_reason: Fixed in f79cca1: day names validated against ValidDays map during config load

title: pgrep -f matches its own process
description: pgrep -f <match> can match the pgrep command itself since the match string appears in its argv. Standard mitigation: use bracket trick in regex or filter own PID. At minimum document this limitation.
close_reason: Fixed in 7979f08: buildPgrepPattern wraps first char in bracket class

title: osascript killType uses match field as app name
description: osascript 'quit app' expects the macOS app name, but match is documented as a pgrep pattern. For most apps these are the same, but not always (e.g. zoom.us vs Zoom). Either add separate appName field, or document the dual-purpose nature of match clearly.
close_reason: Fixed in 7979f08: documented in README that match value is used as osascript app name

title: Full repo review pass
description: Review all code, config, tests, README, and CI in the yappblocker repo. Identify bugs, missing edge cases, code quality issues, inconsistencies, and anything that would embarrass on HN. Create child beads for each issue found.
close_reason: 10 of 11 issues fixed. Remaining: agent-8xs.7 (launchd tests) deferred as enhancement.

title: test issue do not commit
description: probe

title: Missing edge case: start == end means 24/7 active
description: When start equals end (e.g. 09:00/09:00), overnight logic makes it active 24/7 on listed days. Should be documented or validated. Also: empty days list silently never activates.
close_reason: Fixed in 7979f08: documented start==end 24h behavior in README

title: README duplicates brew install command
description: brew install appears in both Installation section and Quick Start step 1. Remove one.
close_reason: Fixed in 7979f08: removed duplicate installation section from README

title: No tests for launchd package
description: internal/launchd has no tests. At minimum test plist template rendering produces valid XML.

title: UserHomeDir errors silently ignored in launchd.go
description: os.UserHomeDir() errors discarded with _. If it fails, paths become /Library/LaunchAgents/ which fails with confusing permission errors. Propagate the error.
close_reason: Fixed in 7979f08: launchd helpers now return (string, error) and propagate UserHomeDir errors
